Washington, Members House Science, Space, Technology Committee today introduced package space bills intended bring stability certainty growing commercial space market.
House Majority Leader Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) introduced Spurring Private Aerospace SPACE alongside Committee Chairman Lamar Smith (R-Texas), Congressman Steven Palazzo (R-Miss.)
additional cosponsors.
Currently, commercial space companies involved activities launching maintaining commercial government satellites, supporting national security assets, contracting NASA's Commercial Cargo Commercial programs support Space Station.
companies testing developing capabilities generation space exploration discovery technologies.
SPACE facilitates pro-growth environment developing commercial space industry improving safety, encouraging private sector investment, creating stable predictable regulatory conditions.
reaffirms FAA's existing ability protect public, national security interests, property foreign policy interests preserves existing authorities related spaceflight participant safety.
Using regulatory backdrop, extends developing commercial spaceflight industry's current learning period structure through 2023.
current learning period expires September.
extended learning period allow better incorporate current activity input commercial space industry.
Other safety provisions would require orbital traffic management report maintain secure environment commercial government assets language encourage protection state launch operator interests.
contains provisions bolster American space companies' launch greater launch license flexibility, among other things.
